project.name: LLM Gateway
project.description: LLM API security proxy gateway — intercepts, scans, and audits all OpenAI-compatible API requests

sprints.name: Phase 13 - Monorepo Restructuring with Turborepo
sprints.goal: Restructure the project into a standard Turborepo monorepo with pnpm workspaces. Gateway and admin become independent apps in apps/ directory, E2E tests move to a separate workspace. Eliminate cross-project config interference and establish proper build pipeline.

features.title: Create root monorepo config files
features.description: Create the foundational monorepo configuration at the project root: (1) Replace root package.json with a workspace root package (name: llm-gateway-monorepo, private: true, turbo devDep, workspace scripts for build/dev/test/typecheck/lint). (2) Create turbo.json defining task pipeline with build dependsOn ["^build"], dev as persistent/uncached, test dependsOn ["build"], typecheck dependsOn ["^build"], lint with no deps. (3) Rewrite pnpm-workspace.yaml to declare packages: ["apps/*", "packages/*"]. (4) Create tsconfig.base.json extracting shared compilerOptions (strict, esModuleInterop, skipLibCheck, forceConsistentCasingInFileNames, resolveJsonModule, declaration, declarationMap, sourceMap). (5) Create .npmrc with shamefully-hoist=true and onlyBuiltDependencies for argon2, better-sqlite3, esbuild. (6) Delete old .npmrc.yml (incorrect format). (7) Create apps/ directory structure (apps/gateway/, apps/admin/, apps/e2e/ as empty dirs).
acceptance_criteria: Given root package.json, when running pnpm install, then workspace packages are recognized
Given turbo.json, when running pnpm turbo build, then tasks execute in dependency order
Given pnpm-workspace.yaml, when running pnpm ls -r, then apps/* packages are listed
Given tsconfig.base.json, when referenced by child tsconfig, then shared options apply
features.technical_notes: Root package.json replaces the current one. Keep packageManager: pnpm@10.18.3. Move pnpm.onlyBuiltDependencies from current gateway package.json to root. The old pnpm-workspace.yaml content (onlyBuiltDependencies: esbuild) is incorrect syntax — this belongs in .npmrc or package.json pnpm field.

features.title: Move gateway to apps/gateway/ and update configs
features.description: Move the entire gateway project into apps/gateway/: (1) Move src/, tests/, migrations/, vitest.config.ts, config.example.toml from root to apps/gateway/. (2) Create apps/gateway/package.json with name @llm-gateway/gateway, moving all current dependencies and scripts from the old root package.json. Keep all fields: type: module, main: dist/index.js, scripts (dev, build, start, test, test:watch, test:coverage, typecheck, lint, clean, gen-key). Remove the pnpm.onlyBuiltDependencies field (moved to root). (3) Create apps/gateway/tsconfig.json extending ../../tsconfig.base.json, keeping gateway-specific options: target ES2024, module Node16, moduleResolution Node16, rootDir src, outDir dist, include ["src/**/*.ts"], types ["node"]. (4) vitest.config.ts stays unchanged — test paths (src/**/*.test.ts, tests/**/*.test.ts) are relative to apps/gateway/ which is correct.
acceptance_criteria: Given apps/gateway/package.json, when running pnpm install from root, then gateway deps are installed
Given apps/gateway/tsconfig.json, when extending tsconfig.base.json, then shared + specific options merge correctly
Given moved vitest.config.ts, when running pnpm test --filter=@llm-gateway/gateway, then tests are discovered from correct paths
Given apps/gateway/src/ structure preserved, when running pnpm build --filter=@llm-gateway/gateway, then tsc compiles successfully
features.technical_notes: Use git mv to preserve history. The critical change is tsconfig.json — it must extend ../../tsconfig.base.json while overriding with Node16 module resolution and ES2024 target. The include path stays ["src/**/*.ts"] since it's relative to apps/gateway/. Do NOT update import paths within src/ — they use relative .js imports which work regardless of directory depth.

features.title: Move admin to apps/admin/ and update configs
features.description: Move the admin project into apps/admin/: (1) Move all admin/ contents (src/, index.html, vite.config.ts, tailwind.config.ts, postcss.config.js, components.json, tsconfig.json, tsconfig.node.json) to apps/admin/. Do NOT move e2e/ directory. (2) Update apps/admin/package.json: change name to @llm-gateway/admin, remove package-lock.json. (3) Update tsconfig.json baseUrl and paths to remain correct (paths alias @/* → ./src/* stays the same since it's relative). (4) vite.config.ts proxy to localhost:3000 stays unchanged. (5) Delete admin/package-lock.json and admin/tsconfig.tsbuildinfo if present.
acceptance_criteria: Given apps/admin/package.json, when running pnpm install from root, then admin deps are installed
Given apps/admin/vite.config.ts unchanged, when running pnpm dev --filter=@llm-gateway/admin, then Vite dev server starts
Given apps/admin/tsconfig.json, when tsc resolves @/* imports, then they map to ./src/* correctly
Given no package-lock.json in apps/admin/, then only root pnpm-lock.yaml exists
features.technical_notes: Delete admin/node_modules/, admin/package-lock.json, admin/tsconfig.tsbuildinfo before/after move — these are generated files. The admin's tsconfig.json uses moduleResolution: bundler which is independent from the base config and should NOT extend tsconfig.base.json (different module system). Same for tsconfig.node.json.

features.title: Create apps/e2e/ workspace with Playwright tests
features.description: Extract E2E tests into a dedicated workspace package: (1) Create apps/e2e/package.json with name @llm-gateway/e2e, private: true, scripts: { test:e2e: playwright test, test:e2e:ui: playwright test --ui }, devDependencies: { @playwright/test: ^1.59.1 }. (2) Move admin/e2e/*.spec.ts files to apps/e2e/admin/ (audit.spec.ts, keys.spec.ts, login.spec.ts, navigation.spec.ts, overview.spec.ts, providers.spec.ts, security.spec.ts, settings.spec.ts). (3) Move admin/e2e/fixtures/ to apps/e2e/admin/fixtures/ (index.ts, admin-server.ts). (4) Move admin/e2e/BUGS.md to apps/e2e/admin/BUGS.md. (5) Move root playwright.config.ts to apps/e2e/playwright.config.ts, update testDir from ./admin/e2e to ./admin, testMatch stays *.spec.ts, outputDir to ./admin/results. (6) CRITICAL: Update apps/e2e/admin/fixtures/admin-server.ts import paths: all ../../../src/... → ../../gateway/src/... and ../../../tests/helpers/... → ../../gateway/tests/helpers/.... This works because tsx resolves relative paths at runtime. (7) Update apps/e2e/admin/fixtures/index.ts if it references any paths.
acceptance_criteria: Given apps/e2e/package.json, when running pnpm install from root, then @playwright/test is installed
Given apps/e2e/playwright.config.ts, when running pnpm test:e2e --filter=@llm-gateway/e2e, then specs are discovered from apps/e2e/admin/
Given admin-server.ts updated imports, when tsx resolves them, then gateway src modules load correctly from ../../gateway/src/
Given all spec files moved, when playwright runs, then all E2E test files are found
features.technical_notes: The import path change in admin-server.ts is the most critical part: from apps/e2e/admin/fixtures/admin-server.ts to apps/gateway/src/... requires ../../gateway/src/... (up from fixtures→admin→e2e→apps, then into gateway/src). Same for tests/helpers: ../../gateway/tests/helpers/.... These are relative file paths resolved by tsx at runtime, not package imports, so pnpm workspace isolation doesn't apply.

features.title: Update serve-admin.ts for monorepo admin/dist path
features.description: Update the serve-admin plugin to resolve admin/dist from the new monorepo layout. Currently: resolve(process.cwd(), "admin", "dist"). In monorepo, when running from apps/gateway/ (dev mode), admin dist is at ../../admin/dist. When running from project root with turbo, or in Docker production, the path differs. Solution: use import.meta.url + fileURLToPath to resolve relative to the source file location. From apps/gateway/src/plugins/serve-admin.ts, the path to apps/admin/dist is ../../../../admin/dist (plugins→src→gateway→apps→admin/dist). Also update the isApiRoute / prefix checks if they reference hardcoded paths. Update serve-admin.test.ts to match new path resolution.
acceptance_criteria: Given serve-admin.ts with new path resolution, when gateway runs from apps/gateway/, then admin/dist is found at ../../admin/dist
Given serve-admin.ts with new path resolution, when gateway runs in Docker production, then admin/dist is accessible
Given updated serve-admin.test.ts, when tests run, then all serve-admin tests pass
Given admin is built (apps/admin/dist exists), when gateway starts, then /admin/ serves the SPA
features.technical_notes: Use: resolve(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '..', '..', '..', '..', 'admin', 'dist'). Add import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url' and import { dirname, resolve } from 'node:path'. For compiled output (dist/), the path from dist/plugins/serve-admin.js to apps/admin/dist is: ../../admin/dist (dist→gateway→apps→admin/dist) = ../../../admin/dist. Consider using process.cwd() approach with monorepo-aware fallback: try ../../admin/dist relative to cwd, then fall back to monorepo root. Actually, simplest: always resolve relative to file location using import.meta.url.

features.title: Update Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml for monorepo build
features.description: Rewrite Dockerfile for Turborepo monorepo: Builder stage: (1) COPY pnpm-lock.yaml pnpm-workspace.yaml turbo.json package.json (root) + apps/gateway/package.json + apps/admin/package.json. (2) RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile. (3) COPY apps/ apps/ + migrations/ to apps/gateway/migrations/ + turbo.json. (4) RUN pnpm turbo build --filter=@llm-gateway/gateway... (builds admin first as gateway dependency, then gateway). Production stage: (1) COPY root + gateway package.json. (2) RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --prod --filter=@llm-gateway/gateway. (3) COPY built dist from both apps: gateway dist + admin dist. (4) COPY migrations/ and config.example.toml. Ensure the admin/dist path is accessible from gateway's perspective (both under /app/apps/). Update docker-compose.yml build context if needed (should remain "." for root context).
acceptance_criteria: Given Dockerfile, when docker build runs, then both admin and gateway are built
Given Dockerfile production stage, when container starts, then gateway serves both API and admin SPA
Given docker-compose.yml, when docker compose up runs, then gateway starts with admin UI accessible at /admin/
Given turbo build order, when gateway builds, then admin build has already completed
features.technical_notes: Key: use turbo --filter to build only needed packages. The --filter=@llm-gateway/gateway... (with trailing ...) means 'gateway and all its dependencies'. In monorepo, migrations/ and config.example.toml live inside apps/gateway/. Docker build context is the repo root. Consider using turbo prune for optimized Docker builds (copies only needed packages).

features.title: Clean up old root files and update AGENTS.md
features.description: Remove old root-level files that have been moved to apps/: (1) Delete root src/ directory (moved to apps/gateway/src/). (2) Delete root tests/ directory (moved to apps/gateway/tests/). (3) Delete root admin/ directory (moved to apps/admin/ and apps/e2e/). (4) Delete root vitest.config.ts (moved to apps/gateway/). (5) Delete root tsconfig.json (replaced by tsconfig.base.json + apps/gateway/tsconfig.json). (6) Delete root playwright.config.ts (moved to apps/e2e/). (7) Delete root .npmrc.yml (replaced by .npmrc). (8) Update .gitignore for monorepo (add .turbo/, ensure apps/*/dist/ patterns). (9) Update AGENTS.md with new monorepo structure, updated commands (pnpm --filter, turbo run), and new directory layout. (10) Ensure pnpm-lock.yaml is at root only.
acceptance_criteria: Given old root files deleted, when listing root directory, then only monorepo config + apps/ + docs/ remain
Given updated .gitignore, when git status runs, then .turbo/ and dist/ directories are ignored
Given updated AGENTS.md, when reading project docs, then monorepo commands and structure are accurate
Given root cleanup complete, when running git status, then only intended changes appear
features.technical_notes: Use git rm for tracked files to preserve history. The root directory after cleanup should contain: apps/, docs/, .agent-harness/, .agents/, .git/, .gitignore, .npmrc, AGENTS.md, Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, config.toml (gitignored), features.json, migrations (if kept at root), opencode.json, package.json, pnpm-lock.yaml, pnpm-workspace.yaml, progress.md, README.md, turbo.json, tsconfig.base.json. Note: migrations/ should be inside apps/gateway/ now.

features.title: Install dependencies and full verification
features.description: Final verification gate: (1) Run pnpm install from root — verify all workspace packages are recognized and deps resolve correctly. (2) Run pnpm turbo build — verify admin builds first (Vite), then gateway builds (tsc). (3) Run pnpm turbo typecheck — verify both packages typecheck. (4) Run pnpm turbo test — verify gateway unit + integration tests pass. (5) Fix any path issues, import errors, or missing dependencies that arise. This is the validation gate that ensures the monorepo restructuring is complete and working.
acceptance_criteria: Given pnpm install runs, when workspace resolves, then all 3 packages (@llm-gateway/gateway, @llm-gateway/admin, @llm-gateway/e2e) are installed
Given pnpm turbo build runs, when admin build completes, then apps/admin/dist/ contains built assets
Given pnpm turbo build runs, when gateway build completes, then apps/gateway/dist/ contains compiled JS
Given pnpm turbo typecheck runs, then 0 TypeScript errors across all packages
Given pnpm turbo test runs, then all gateway unit + integration tests pass
Given pnpm dev --filter=@llm-gateway/gateway runs, when gateway starts, then /health endpoint responds
features.technical_notes: Run: pnpm install && pnpm turbo build && pnpm turbo typecheck && pnpm turbo test. The first pnpm install may take time as it regenerates the lockfile for the new workspace structure. If tests fail due to path issues, fix and re-run. The turbo cache will speed up subsequent runs.
